title: Ghana national grid emission factor
description: CO2 emitted per kWh supplied into Ghana's national grid. Extracted from the Energy Commission's official statistics, resolving the data gap noted in RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_PLAN.md (previously only a stale IEA-2011 / Climatiq estimate of 0.2629 was available). Corroborated independently: Ghana's 2024 generation mix is 61% thermal / 39% hydro (same bulletin, S3.2), which computes to ~0.35-0.39 at low voltage after ~20% T&D losses. ecoinvent 3.11's GH low-voltage dataset characterises to only ~0.16, a hydro-dominant vintage that predates the thermal shift; engine/grid_calibration.py corrects for this. Note: the Energy Commission value is a CDM-style grid emission factor that converges with the attributional average under thermal dominance.

all_other_projects.use_for: General grid-electricity consumption accounting (e.g. a processing facility drawing from the grid).

wind_solar_projects.use_for: Projects that DISPLACE grid electricity (e.g. solar drying); the combined-margin factor per CDM-style methodology.

provenance.span: Grid emission factor is the amount of CO2 emitted per unit of electricity generated and supplied into the national electricity grid. Row 'Grid Emission Factor (all other projects)', 2024 column = 0.35 tCO2/MWh; 'Grid Emission Factor (wind/solar projects)', 2024 = 0.32 tCO2/MWh.
provenance.publication: 2025 Energy Statistics, Energy Commission of Ghana
provenance.licence: Ghana govt publication; reuse terms not formally confirmed (cite; see LICENCES.md)
provenance.extraction_caveat: Column-to-year alignment was inferred from a flattened -layout extraction (2017 and 2018 columns appear blank). Verify the 2024 value against the source table visually before using it in published figures.
